name: Project.Commander.TelnetTab.Feature_MonospaceFormatting
observations: Telnet tab output widget now uses monospace font (Consolas 9pt, fallback Courier New) to preserve ASCII table formatting from FBC/RPC/LOG command outputs.
Implementation: Set QFont with Monospace StyleHint on QTextEdit widget in TelnetTab._setup_ui() method to ensure equal character spacing for tabular data alignment.
User requirement: ASCII tables from fieldbus commands (FBC IO structure prints) must maintain column/row alignment when displayed in Telnet tab for readability.

name: Project.Commander.TelnetTab.Method_SetMonospaceFont
observations: Method location: TelnetTab._setup_ui() in src/commander/ui/telnet_tab.py - configures output widget font after QTextEdit creation before adding to layout.

Font selection strategy: Primary Consolas 9pt (Windows standard monospace), exactMatch() check for availability, fallback to Courier New 9pt if unavailable, StyleHint.Monospace for system-appropriate font.
Integration: Added QFont import from PyQt6.QtGui, applied font via self.output.setFont(monospace_font) immediately after output widget instantiation.

name: Global.UI.Pattern_MonospaceFontForTables
observations: Pattern: Configure QTextEdit widgets displaying tabular/ASCII art data with monospace fonts to preserve character alignment regardless of character width variations in proportional fonts.
Technique: Use QFont.StyleHint.Monospace + explicit font family (Consolas/Courier New) + exactMatch() validation to ensure cross-platform monospace rendering in Qt text widgets.
Use case: Terminal outputs, log displays, ASCII tables, code snippets - any content where character positioning determines meaning (columns, indentation, box drawing).
